changes.reason: Keep the proven charm-pin tail box focused on the worst remaining tail: the -527 MAE trade had rolling-high distance 14.75, while the current 15 boundary only barely includes it. Testing 14.75 checks whether exact boundary capture is enough without widening the box further.

The worst charm-pin tail entered extremely extended above VWAP at 26.20 points, while protected extended-VWAP winners have broad overlap. Raising the tail-risk signature's minimum VWAP distance to 20 makes this a profile-scoped high-extension tail test rather than a broad extended-VWAP veto.

The worst charm-pin row had negative VWAP acceleration (-0.015), matching a stall/rejection state. Keeping the max acceleration at 0 preserves the refined tail box around non-accelerating extension and avoids blocking clean accelerating charm-pin recoveries.

Prior accepted charm-pin tail work used 60 successfully. Keep the floor at 60 so this experiment is driven by the extension/rolling-high tail geometry, not by a broad setup-quality filter that the report shows would over-prune.

expected_effect: Should reduce or isolate the remaining -527 charm_pin_pullback_long tail while preserving most fast charm-pin winners. Tradeoff is a small loss of profitable extended charm-pin recoveries if the high-extension box overlaps more than expected; reject if total PnL retention falls below 98% or fast_under_15m drops by more than 3.
label: charm-pin-high-extension-tail-box
rationale: Use one surgical-veto/runtime-risk family: refine the already accepted charm-pin tail-risk box instead of repeating failed 1DTE, fallback, call-wall, or broad entry-pruning experiments. The current leader's main defect is hidden heat, and the worst trade is a VALID_0DTE charm_pin_pullback_long threshold-box case: VWAP distance 26.20, rolling-high distance 14.75, pullback 9.75, weak RSI slope, ADX under 18, and -527 MAE. Prior charm-pin tail-box changes improved score and net profit, so this is the highest-evidence next move, but scoped tightly to avoid blocking protected extended-VWAP winners.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: charm_pin_pullback_long contributes $1237.7 over 34 trades with 5.3 minute median hold and average MAE only -74.3, but owns the worst -527 MAE. The worst row is high-extension/non-accelerating above VWAP with large pullback and rolling-high distance, while broad extended-VWAP filters would remove protected winners.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Charm Pin High-Extension Tail Box
strategy_hypotheses.risk: The same high-extension state can recover profitably; if the box captures too many charm-pin winners, score and total net profit will fall despite better MAE.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: VALID_0DTE charm_pin_pullback_long entry with high ES-VWAP extension, non-positive VWAP acceleration, large rolling-high/pullback distance, and setup quality above the tail-risk floor.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Worst MAE improves from -527 toward <= -403, charm_pin_pullback_long retains at least 90% of profile PnL, total net profit remains at least 98% of run_001, and fast_under_15m trades fall by no more than 3.

strategy_plan_reference.market_regime_read: The current leader is a favorable long-only momentum/pullback sample: 107 trades, $7125.8 PnL, 100% realized win rate, 0.4% drawdown, no slow >=12h trades, and 65 fast under-15m...
